mnemonics: Proof of Work: People Often Want Fabulous Riches (POW: computing power for rewards)
Delegated Proof of Stake: Don't Pick Strangers, Only Friends (DPoS: vote for trusted validators)
Byzantine Generals: Betraying Generals Lie, Always Trust Original Messages (BFT: agreement despite traitors)
keyPoints: Consensus mechanisms ensure all nodes in a decentralized network agree on a single, valid version of the transaction history.
Different mechanisms (e.g., Proof of Work, Proof of Stake, Byzantine Fault Tolerance) achieve consensus through varying trade-offs between security, decentralization, and scalability.
The mechanism is fundamental to blockchain security, preventing double-spending and maintaining network integrity without a central authority.
commonMistakes: Believing Proof of Work is only about creating new coins (its primary purpose is securing the network through expensive computation).
Assuming all consensus mechanisms are equally decentralized (e.g., DPoS can lead to a smaller, elected set of validators, increasing centralization risk).
Confusing the Byzantine Generals Problem with a simple voting system (it's about reaching reliable consensus in the presence of malicious actors or faulty nodes).
